What force moves the nations?
Biographical historians and historians of separate nations understand this force as a power inherent in heroes and rulers.

While Pierre was repeating what he had been told about the army leaving Moscow, a thin, sallow, tattered French soldier came up to the door of the shed.
Pierre saw that Platón did not want to understand what the Frenchman was saying, and he looked on without interfering.
Occasionally he glanced at the familiar crowd around him and then again at his feet.
Mentally addressing the rain, he repeated: “Now then, now then, go on!
When Pierre reached the fire and heard Platón’s voice enfeebled by illness, and saw his pathetic face brightly lit up by the blaze, he felt a painful prick at his heart.
